title: Reservation authorization and capacity workbench for Dedicated pools
preview: Goal: Define and implement a bounded, auditable reservation request and approval workflow plus an operator workbench that ties throughput guarantees, autoscaling policy templates, and fleet controls into the Dedicated provisioning lifecycle. This ticket scopes the PM deliverables: request form schema, approval matri...
text:
Goal: Define and implement a bounded, auditable reservation request and approval workflow plus an operator workbench that ties throughput guarantees, autoscaling policy templates, and fleet controls into the Dedicated provisioning lifecycle. This ticket scopes the PM deliverables: request form schema, approval matrix, capacity planning heuristics, UX sketches for the workbench, and the verification playbook for provisioning events.

Context: Several early customers are adopting Dedicated and ask for a single place to request guaranteed throughput (tokens/sec or requests/sec), choose autoscaling behaviors (aggressive, balanced, conservative), and indicate fleet constraints (allowed regions, GPU families, maintenance windows). Engineers want predictable capacity sizing and test hooks to validate SLOs after an allocation. Current ad-hoc requests cause back-and-forth between sales, SRE, a...

title: Contributor stewardship, deprecation & retirement playbook for design system
preview: Establish a single, pragmatic playbook for accepting contributions to the design system, lifecycle review for components and tokens, and a safe deprecation/retirement process. This ticket consolidates prior ad-hoc guidance into a playbook that: 1) makes contribution expectations clear, 2) defines an RFC + review cad...
text: Establish a single, pragmatic playbook for accepting contributions to the design system, lifecycle review for components and tokens, and a safe deprecation/retirement process. This ticket consolidates prior ad-hoc guidance into a playbook that: 1) makes contribution expectations clear, 2) defines an RFC + review cadence for breaking changes, 3) defines telemetry and usage thresholds for safe retirement, and 4) gives migration guidance for tokens and components. Goal: reduce friction for contributors while making risk visible to product and infra stakeholders.

title: Synthetic Load Validator and Reservation Autocalibration Playbook
preview:
Summary:
During a capacity incident on 2025-02-10 we observed degraded throughput across several Dedicated clusters. Investigation pointed to a combination of: (1) under-provisioned tenant reservations due to stale traffic estimates, (2) noisy-neighbor workloads that generated burst token exhaustion, and (3) burst p...
text:
Summary:
During a capacity incident on 2025-02-10 we observed degraded throughput across several Dedicated clusters. Investigation pointed to a combination of: (1) under-provisioned tenant reservations due to stale traffic estimates, (2) noisy-neighbor workloads that generated burst token exhaustion, and (3) burst policy thresholds that allowed aggressive short-term bursts without adequate backoff, leading to cascading latency spikes.

Goal:
Create a reproducible synthetic load validator and an automated reservation autocalibration playbook that: (a) reproduces the incident class with controlled noisy tenants and realistic KV/cache utilization, (b) derives corrective reservation deltas, and (c) produces safe degraded-burst fallback policies that reduce tail latency without unduly impacting throughput.

Scope and non-goals:
- Scope: Dedicated cluster behavior, tenant reservation sizing...

title: Mailbox probe + drift-aware adaptive discard for low-cost trace capture
preview: Background: Our current tracing hotpath collects a large volume of spans for inference requests which increases tail allocations and CPU in the hotpath. Existing work reduced noise via prefilters and deferred emission, but we still see cases where small bursts and model drift cause missed high-value traces or unnece...
text:
Background: Our current tracing hotpath collects a large volume of spans for inference requests which increases tail allocations and CPU in the hotpath. Existing work reduced noise via prefilters and deferred emission, but we still see cases where small bursts and model drift cause missed high-value traces or unnecessary allocations for low-value traces.

Goal: Implement a lightweight, mailbox-style probe that opportunistically samples contextual headers and a drift-aware adaptive discard stage that drops low-value tracing tokens outside the mailbox budget. The mailbox probe must add near-zero allocations in the hotpath and enable accurate tail reconstruction (posthoc) while keeping overall tracer CPU and memory overhead under the target SLO (<=0.5% CPU on inference path).

Approach:
1) Mailbox probe (hotpath): implement a tiny fixed-size circular metadata mailbox per worker thread th...

title: Tenant credit-based region routing with variant shadowing and graceful hosted backfill
preview:
Summary:
Design and implement a tenant credit allocator that influences region & tier selection for dedicated capacity while enabling controlled hosted spillback. The allocator issues soft "credits" (soft reservations) based on pre-commit, historical consumption, and SLA tier. Credits drive probabilistic routing dec...
text:
Summary:
Design and implement a tenant credit allocator that influences region & tier selection for dedicated capacity while enabling controlled hosted spillback. The allocator issues soft "credits" (soft reservations) based on pre-commit, historical consumption, and SLA tier. Credits drive probabilistic routing decisions: prefer dedicated when credits >= cost_estimate, gradually bleed to hosted when credits are low.

Goals:
- Reduce unexpected hosted spillovers for customers with reserved capacity while allowing controlled, observable backfill to hosted for short bursts.
- Maintain latency SLOs for P0/P1 tenants and keep unit cost predictable for reserved customers.
- Provide a transparent, debuggable fallback mechanism to hosted and to lower-cost model variants.

Design highlights:
1) Credit allocator
- Each tenant has a credit balance updated continuously: credits += reserved_rate ...

title: Adaptive Pool Tuner: Predictive Cost & Latency Tradeoff Canvas
preview: Problem: Operators of dedicated GPU pools need a fast way to tune autoscale and burst settings while understanding the expected cost and latency tradeoffs for the observed workload. Current pool controls are distributed across multiple panels; users must toggle between health, scaling policy, and cost dashboards to ...
text:
Problem: Operators of dedicated GPU pools need a fast way to tune autoscale and burst settings while understanding the expected cost and latency tradeoffs for the observed workload. Current pool controls are distributed across multiple panels; users must toggle between health, scaling policy, and cost dashboards to make a single configuration change. This leads to conservative settings, overprovisioning, and surprise bills.

Goal: Design a single, compact canvas — the Adaptive Pool Tuner — that surfaces predicted tail latency, median latency, and incremental cost for proposed autoscale and burst rule changes. The canvas should support short iterative workflows: propose change, simulate effect on a chosen workload profile, preview cost delta and SLO impact, save as draft or apply with rollout policy.

Scope:
- A compact left rail with workload selector (recent routes, custom trace uplo...

title: Signal Flow Navigator — lineage and lightweight request exploration
preview: Problem: Engineers need a fast, low-friction way to explore request lineage across services without the cognitive overhead of a full-forensics trace workspace. Current consoles focus on dense timelines and span-level forensic tools; customers asked for a lightweight explorer that surfaces end-to-end causality, corre...
text:
Problem: Engineers need a fast, low-friction way to explore request lineage across services without the cognitive overhead of a full-forensics trace workspace. Current consoles focus on dense timelines and span-level forensic tools; customers asked for a lightweight explorer that surfaces end-to-end causality, correlation IDs, and cost/latency overlays for quick debugging and rollout validation.

Goal: Design a compact, keyboard-first 'Signal Flow Navigator' that lets users: 1) search by correlation ID or route, 2) see a condensed lineage graph + linear time-strip, 3) toggle span-grouping and sampling, and 4) overlay per-request cost and latency heatmarks. It must integrate with rollouts so PM/SEs can validate canaries and quickly escalate or rollback.

Scope: UX surface + clickable prototype (Figma) for console team review; include acceptance criteria, accessibility considerations, t...

title: Latency-Adaptive Phase Sharding with Kernel-Cost Feedback
preview: Problem: In mixed workloads our current phase-splitting heuristics are either too aggressive (increasing overhead and hurting throughput) or too conservative (causing p95/p99 latency spikes for low-latency requests). We lack a tight feedback loop from kernel-level cost signals (measured FLOPs, attention memory hotsp...
text:
Problem: In mixed workloads our current phase-splitting heuristics are either too aggressive (increasing overhead and hurting throughput) or too conservative (causing p95/p99 latency spikes for low-latency requests). We lack a tight feedback loop from kernel-level cost signals (measured FLOPs, attention memory hotspots, quantization path selection) back into the scheduler, so the allocator cannot make fine-grained sharding decisions that balance latency and throughput at the token-phase level.

Goal: Introduce a scheduler policy that shards large prefill/decode phases into adaptive micro-shards using real-time kernel-cost feedback. The policy should: (1) reduce tail latency for latency-sensitive requests without materially lowering sustained throughput, (2) avoid pathological fragmentation overhead, and (3) be safe to roll out per-customer and per-route with canarying and automatic ro...

title: Endpoint behavioral SLI spec and graceful capacity-shedding guidelines
preview: Create a pragmatic, behavioral SLI specification for model endpoints that maps to observable request behaviors (short vs long context, streaming vs non-streaming) and define a multi-tiered capacity-shedding policy that preserves high-priority traffic using graceful degradation, model fallback, regional evacuation, a...
text: Create a pragmatic, behavioral SLI specification for model endpoints that maps to observable request behaviors (short vs long context, streaming vs non-streaming) and define a multi-tiered capacity-shedding policy that preserves high-priority traffic using graceful degradation, model fallback, regional evacuation, and circuit breakers. The aim is to give on-call and platform automation a clear decision tree to protect error budgets while minimizing customer-visible regressions.

title: Token Waterfall Slate: compact attribution + export patterns
preview: Design and prototype a compact, high-density "Waterfall Slate" for the Redwood Console cost dashboard that surfaces token-level attribution (per-route, per-model, per-invocation) and supports multi-format export. The slate should present a layered token-cost waterfall (origin layers: prompt, completion, KV cache hit...
text: Design and prototype a compact, high-density "Waterfall Slate" for the Redwood Console cost dashboard that surfaces token-level attribution (per-route, per-model, per-invocation) and supports multi-format export. The slate should present a layered token-cost waterfall (origin layers: prompt, completion, KV cache hit, prefix reuse, retries), allow quick pivoting by dimension (route, workspace, model family, quantization profile), and provide guided pricing-simulation controls (unit rate overrides, slab presets, reserved capacity adjustments). The goal is a single-pane summary that is readable for both engineers and finance stakeholders, with progressive disclosure for forensic detail and an export format suited for downstream chargeback and BI ingestion.

title: Adaptive FlowCards and Responsive Token Synthesis for Rollout Console
preview: Objective: Create a single FlowCard component family and a responsive token synthesis approach that adapts density, contrast, and motion across rollouts dashboards. The goal is to reduce component sprawl in the Console, enable consistent canary/A-B rollout UIs, and make token-driven behavior (spacing, elevation, ani...
text: Objective: Create a single FlowCard component family and a responsive token synthesis approach that adapts density, contrast, and motion across rollouts dashboards. The goal is to reduce component sprawl in the Console, enable consistent canary/A-B rollout UIs, and make token-driven behavior (spacing, elevation, animation duration) predictable for engineering handoff. This ticket drives a Figma library delivery, accessibility pass, and a small prototype to validate interaction under live data loads.

title: Catalog pricing matrix + deprecation banners — screenshot & quickstart visual pack for release notes
preview: Produce a focused set of UX assets and high-fidelity screenshots for the Feb catalog release notes: primary deliverables are a pricing matrix visualization (desktop + mobile + dark mode), model variant rows (standard/quantized/fast), deprecation banners and the migration quickstart modal. These will be used in the p...
text: Produce a focused set of UX assets and high-fidelity screenshots for the Feb catalog release notes: primary deliverables are a pricing matrix visualization (desktop + mobile + dark mode), model variant rows (standard/quantized/fast), deprecation banners and the migration quickstart modal. These will be used in the public release notes, the Console release banner, and the onboarding quickstart flow that surfaces newly added or deprecated models. The designs must be consistent with the refreshed catalog visual language and include explicit cost/tooling callouts (per-token and estimated hour cost), region availability badges, and an accessible deprecation CTA flow.

title: Dark-mode interaction ink reflow and graph legend hierarchy
preview: Problem: In deep-dark console themes interactive elements (buttons, menus, toggles) exhibit visual artifacts and perceived weight due to 'ink' reflow and overlay rules. Chart legends and color keys lose hierarchy against near-black surfaces, and dense tables depend on thin separators that drop below readable contras...
text: Problem: In deep-dark console themes interactive elements (buttons, menus, toggles) exhibit visual artifacts and perceived weight due to 'ink' reflow and overlay rules. Chart legends and color keys lose hierarchy against near-black surfaces, and dense tables depend on thin separators that drop below readable contrast at small sizes. Scope: Create a token mapping and component override proposal for deep/dense dark surfaces that preserves accessible contrast, preserves brand accent identity, and avoids visual clutter across tables, charts, and controls. Deliverables: 1) Token transform rules (alpha overlays, elevation-strength multipliers, accent-saturation clamp) 2) Component override spec for Button / Menu / Toggle / Table row / Chart legend 3) Figma tokens and component variants 4) Implementation guidance for theme engine (runtime mapping + opt-in canary) 5) Automated contrast tests ...

title: Incident-aware Upgrade Schedule Editor for Private Clusters
preview: Problem: Operators of private/VPC clusters need a single, actionable place in the Console to schedule cluster upgrades while taking active incidents, maintenance windows, and customer SLOs into account. Current upgrade scheduling is too static: it doesn't show ongoing incidents, doesn't suggest safe canary windows, ...
text:
Problem: Operators of private/VPC clusters need a single, actionable place in the Console to schedule cluster upgrades while taking active incidents, maintenance windows, and customer SLOs into account. Current upgrade scheduling is too static: it doesn't show ongoing incidents, doesn't suggest safe canary windows, and lacks a clear rollback/abort affordance for mid-upgrade incidents.

Goal: Design an interactive Upgrade Schedule Editor that: 1) surfaces current incident state and cluster health when picking times; 2) suggests safe windows based on SLA/SLO and past busy-hours telemetry; 3) supports canary waves, staged rollouts, and quick abort/rollback actions; 4) integrates RBAC so only authorized personas can change critical windows; and 5) provides clear audit trail + links to incident runbooks.

Scope of this ticket: UX prototypes and validation for the Editor (desktop & compact ...

title: Privileged role grant review & audit UX for Console
preview: Problem: Admins need a clear, low-friction way to grant, review, and revoke privileged roles (e.g., system-admin, billing-admin, oncall-privileged) while ensuring every change is auditable and can be justified for compliance. Current console path buries audit metadata and the member assignment flow creates accidenta...
text:
Problem: Admins need a clear, low-friction way to grant, review, and revoke privileged roles (e.g., system-admin, billing-admin, oncall-privileged) while ensuring every change is auditable and can be justified for compliance. Current console path buries audit metadata and the member assignment flow creates accidental over-grants because scopes and resource boundaries are not surfaced.

Goal: Design a compact role grant review surface in Console that: 1) surfaces resource scoping and active sessions for the member being assigned; 2) flags high-risk grants with inline justifications and mandatory commit notes for P0/P1 roles; 3) integrates with audit log links and one-click revoke/impersonate for validation; 4) preserves quick bulk workflows for SRE/platform teams while preventing accidental permanent elevation.

Scope: UI/UX for role editor and member assignment flows only (Console). N...

title: Onboarding network topology journey frames
preview: Create a set of visual assets and annotated UI frames that guide operators through Private/Private-network onboarding end-to-end: topology overview, initial install steps, control-plane handshake, RBAC & key setup, and in-place upgrade flows. Assets will be used in the Console onboarding flow, docs quickstart, and r...
text: Create a set of visual assets and annotated UI frames that guide operators through Private/Private-network onboarding end-to-end: topology overview, initial install steps, control-plane handshake, RBAC & key setup, and in-place upgrade flows. Assets will be used in the Console onboarding flow, docs quickstart, and release notes for runtime-1.18.

title: Pricing commitment matrix & security questionnaire alignment for SSO/RBAC contract variability
preview: Run a pricing/packaging experiment and create canonical security-questionnaire answers tying SSO (SAML/SCIM) and RBAC commitments to contractual packaging. Goal: reduce negotiation friction, standardize commitments across tiers, and measure lift in close rate and deal size when we offer clearly-scoped SSO/RBAC commi...
text: Run a pricing/packaging experiment and create canonical security-questionnaire answers tying SSO (SAML/SCIM) and RBAC commitments to contractual packaging. Goal: reduce negotiation friction, standardize commitments across tiers, and measure lift in close rate and deal size when we offer clearly-scoped SSO/RBAC commitments vs bespoke offers.

title: Batching & Cache Setup Wizard — onboarding flow and safety guardrails
preview: Design an end-to-end onboarding wizard inside Redwood Console that helps engineers and platform owners configure batching and KV/prefix caching for routes and model profiles. The wizard should: 1) quickly recommend safe defaults based on measured workload signals (median seq len, token rate, request size distributio...
text: Design an end-to-end onboarding wizard inside Redwood Console that helps engineers and platform owners configure batching and KV/prefix caching for routes and model profiles. The wizard should: 1) quickly recommend safe defaults based on measured workload signals (median seq len, token rate, request size distribution), 2) explain trade-offs (latency vs cost vs variance) with short contextual explainers, 3) provide a simulated cost & latency preview for chosen presets and slider settings, 4) expose advanced controls in a scoped ‘expert’ panel with JSON preview and validation, and 5) enforce guardrails and warnings for settings that would violate SLOs or exceed estimated spend. This ticket covers the visual flows, microcopy, onboarding checklist, one progressive disclosure prototype in Figma, and a short pilot usability test with 6 backend engineers and 4 ML infra users.

title: Surface-elevation press shadows and RTL icon offsets: cross-theme button parity
preview: Audit and implement a coherent approach to button press feedback (shadows/contrast) that respects surface elevation tokens across light, dark, and high-contrast themes, and fix inconsistent RTL icon offsets and hit targets introduced during the recent token migration. Deliver a small set of token mappings, design sp...
text: Audit and implement a coherent approach to button press feedback (shadows/contrast) that respects surface elevation tokens across light, dark, and high-contrast themes, and fix inconsistent RTL icon offsets and hit targets introduced during the recent token migration. Deliver a small set of token mappings, design specs, and a developer-friendly migration guide so engineering can implement with minimal regressions.

title: Performance Signalboard: model & route overview canvas
preview: Goal: Design a compact, scannable ‘Performance Signalboard’ canvas that surfaces per-model and per-route runtime signals (latency percentiles, throughput, token metrics, cost per token) for devs and platform engineers. The canvas should work as a route-level default view and be embeddable in model pages and rollout ...
text:
Goal: Design a compact, scannable ‘Performance Signalboard’ canvas that surfaces per-model and per-route runtime signals (latency percentiles, throughput, token metrics, cost per token) for devs and platform engineers. The canvas should work as a route-level default view and be embeddable in model pages and rollout screens.

Scope:
- Primary surface: single-row cards for routes/models with sparkline + trend + KPI badges.
- Expandable detail panel to show percentile charts (p50/p95/p99), tokens-per-call distribution, and request concurrency heatmap.
- Cost overlay that estimates per-1k tokens and per-1000-requests for the selected timeframe.
- Rollout annotations: highlight canary windows and automatic fallback events on timelines.
- Interaction patterns: hover tooltips, time-range zoom, selectable percentile lines, and quick copy of telemetry queries.

Out of scope for this ticket: in...
